docs.title: Home
docs.text: Welcome to documentation for Yggdrasil toolkit for Godot 4. Please check Getting Started for first steps!

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install Yggdrasil plugin either use Asset Store or download latest release from GitHub. Then enable it in Project Settings > Plugins

Godot version

Plugin requires Godot version 4.7+

docs.title: Creating first tree
docs.text: When the plugin is enabled, new tab called Yggdrasil at the top will be created. Press it to enter Yggdrasil editor. What you see right now is the registry browser, this is where all your trees will be listed. Right now it is empty so we have to create new tree, but to do that, you need a Group.

docs.title: What is a group?
docs.text: For organization and identification purpose, Yggdrasil uses Groups as parents for Trees. Every group name has to have unique name, every tree in a group has to have unique name but trees in different groups can share names. Outside of organization and loading trees, Groups don't hold any special functionality so you don't have to worry about them too much.

docs.title: Creating new group
docs.text: It is as simple as opening Group menu in the top left and pressing Create or using shortcut CTRL (Cmd ⌘ on Mac) + B. New entry will be added and focus for edit, so type the name of your first group and press Enter.

docs.title: Creating tree
docs.text:
Now that we have Group created, make sure it is selected and then from Tree menu select Create or use shortcute CTRL (Cmd ⌘ on Mac) + N. Just like with our Group, you have to name your new tree.

Let's say that our registry now looks like this:

Spells
└─ Fire

Spells being the group and Fire the tree.

docs.title: Editing a tree
docs.text: Double click (or Enter when selected) your Tree entry to open new tab with the tree editor.

docs.title: Validator
docs.text: System runs validation on the tree whenever any change occurs. You will notice two icons in the bottom right corner. When you press them, info panels will appear above with a message. You don't really have to worry about that right now, initial warnings and errors are fixed on the go.

docs.title: Adding new nodes
docs.text: Right-click on the middle panel to open context menu. From there select New Node → Small. This will add new node, which right now looks like a white square, that's the default node icon that comes with Yggdrasil.

docs.title: Manipulating nodes
docs.text: There are 2 tools available Select (Q) and Move (W). In Select mode, you can select individual nodes or multiple by dragging with left-click. In Move mode you can move selected nodes around by draggin with left-click. Every other property can be changed in the Inspector tab on the right side, but more on that later.

docs.title: Connecting nodes
docs.text: Go ahead and create one more node. Now select one of them, then while holding Shift, click on the second node. This should create a line between them. You can change line texture in Settings tab on the right side. You can also change line style in Inspector when you select node that has outgoing connection. There are 3 styles: Straight, Bezier and Arc. Try them out.

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text:
In the Attributes tab panel, you can add new attributes that can be then assigned to nodes. Press the button with plus sign to add new attribute entry. Type the name and press enter. Attribute data consists of:

attribute_id - this is what nodes use and what you will use in your scripts (like additional_life)
├── Name - this is for you to use
├── Effect - this is used mostly in tooltips, you can use character '#' as a placeholder for values, there should be as many '#' as the Value count
└── Value count - for the Effect and Array to hold values

Create new Life attribute:

additional_life
├── Name: Life
├── Effect: +# to maximum Life
└── Value count: 1

Now if you select any node, there will be checkbox next to the attribute id. So select one of your nodes and then mark that checkbox. If you go back to Inspector tab, you will notice new panel appeared called Attributes. There you will see your attribute:

additional_life
└── Value 1: 0

Double-click that Value 1 entry and set it to 10. Now if you hover over that node with your mouse, you should see "+10 to maximum Life".

docs.title: Allocation
docs.text: If tree has Allocation enabled in Settings, players will be able to interact with nodes by pressing them like a button. This however requires at least one Root node. This is also one of the errors that Validator throws. To mark a node as Root, select that node and in Inspector check Is Root. That's it, now that node is a Root and so will be the first node that players have to select. There can be multiple Root nodes, if you want multiple starting points or have multiple branches that are not connected.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: You can change background color or set a tiling texture in the Settings tab.

docs.title: Lines
docs.text: You can also set textures for connecting lines there.

docs.title: Node icon
docs.text: For nodes, you will have to prepare spritesheet that will be used to select icons. If you have image that has 10x10 small icons of size 32x32px, open Settings tab and under Icon Size panel change Small Node inputs to be X: 32 and Y: 32. This is all that you have to change, script will do the rest. Now select one of your small nodes, go to Inspector tab and under Visuals panel where Icon row is, press the Texture2D picker button (it's the one with the File icon). That will open new small window that doesn't really have anything. We have to select our spritesheet file. Press the same looking icon in that window and then pick your spritesheet. Now you should see individual icons, you can select one of them. Your node now has own icon!

docs.title: Node border
docs.text:
If you are going to use borders, you can select individual images in the Inspector under Visuals panel. There are 3 states: Normal, Intermediate and Active (same goes for lines).

  • Normal is selected by default
  • Intermediate is selected when node is accessible and can be allocated (neighbor of allocated node or is a Root node)
  • Active is selected when node is allocated

docs.title: Interaction
docs.text:
As you probably already noticed, in the Settings tab there is a panel for Interaction. This is used to choose how players interact with the tree.

  • Revealed is used to show or hide nodes based on the allocation, allocated and intermediate nodes are visible while everything else is hidden
  • Allocation is used to allow players to instantly allocate and deallocate nodes without any confirmation
  • Pre-allocation is used to allow players to first preallocate nodes and then confirm that allocation, same for deallocation, without submition, changes will be lost, preallocated nodes are not the same as allocated nodes. Requires Allocation to be enabled.

docs.title: Version
docs.text: In the Settings tab you can also notice Version input. You should increase this number every time you make drastic changes to your tree (e.g. connections change or nodes are removed), when player loads tree with version other than current tree version, tree_version_mismatch signal will be emitted, you can connect to it and completely wipe players tree in that case.

docs.title: Prefabs
docs.text: Recreating the same node multiple times can get very annoying. This is why there is a Prefabs system.

docs.title: Save as Prefab
docs.text: Select any node(s), right-click to open context menu as select Save as Prefab. Now if you look at the bottom bar, there are several tabs, depending on which type of node you just saved, prefabs will be stored there. So press on that tab and you will notice a file-like node there. That's the prefab, you can now drag and drop it into the view. This newly created node is the same that you just saved as a prefab, but there is more to it.

docs.title: What are prefabs?
docs.text: When you save node as a prefab, reference is created, meaning that every node that shares that reference also shares properties. If you change the name of one of these nodes, every other node will change as well. Nodes duplicated in view will also duplicate prefab reference.

docs.title: Save as Copy
docs.text: You might want to also reuse a node without keeping the reference, that's what copies are. Selecting Save as Copy from context menu, will add new entry to the bottom tabs. Prefabs and Copies have little icon in their corner so it's easier to tell which is which.

docs.title: Make Unique
docs.text: Nodes created from prefabs can be turned into normal nodes by selecting Make Unique from the context menu. This breaks the prefab reference while keeping current node properties.

docs.title: Runtime
docs.text: Runtime scripts are used at runtime only.

docs.title: Saving and Loading tree state
docs.text: When YggdrasilTree is built using YggdrasilBuilder, it will also load YggdrasilTreeState from user://yggdrasil/{uid}.tree.

docs.title: Saving tree state
docs.text:
While loading is automated, saving on the other hand needs to be controlled by you. Not to worry, this can be done with a single line of code.

func _on_save_button_pressed():
    YggdrasilSerializer.save_tree_state(_tree)

docs.title: Shared
docs.text: Shared scripts are used both by the editor and at runtime.

docs.title: Using trees in-game
docs.text: If you haven't, check Getting Started first.

docs.title: Loading YggdrasilTree
docs.text:
To get YggdrasilTree resource, use YggdrasilLoader.load_tree(path: String).

In Getting Started we have created Group called Spells and Tree called Fire.

Spells
└─ Fire

To get YggdrasilTree for our Fire tree, use YggdrasilLoader.load_tree("Spells/Fire").

func _ready():
    var tree: YggdrasilTree = YggdrasilLoader.load_tree("Spells/Tree")
    # ...

docs.title: Building YggdrasilTree
docs.text:
Now that we have our tree resource loaded, we can build it and place in our UI.

For that we will use YggdrasilBuilder. For full API documentation check YggdrasilBuilder. For now we are interested only in YggdrasilBuilder.new(tree: YggdrasilTree), YggdrasilBuilder.set_parent(new_parent: Node) and YggdrasilBuilder.build().

First we will initialize our builder

func _ready():
    var tree: YggdrasilTree = YggdrasilLoader.load_tree("Spells/Tree")
    var builder: YggdrasilBuilder = YggdrasilBuilder.new(tree)
    # ...

Then we have to provide a parent Node, this is where YggdrasilTreeView will be added. It's recommended to use Node of type Control (can be inherited) with clip_contents set to true (we don't need the tree view to be visible outside the parent).

@export var tree_parent: Control

func _ready():
    var tree: YggdrasilTree = YggdrasilLoader.load_tree("Spells/Tree")
    var builder: YggdrasilBuilder = YggdrasilBuilder.new(tree)
    builder.set_parent(tree_parent)
    # ...

Now all that is left to do is build the tree and get created YggdrasilTreeView.

@export var tree_parent: Control

var _tree_view: YggdrasilTreeView

func _ready():
    var tree: YggdrasilTree = YggdrasilLoader.load_tree("Spells/Tree")
    var builder: YggdrasilBuilder = YggdrasilBuilder.new(tree)
    builder.set_parent(tree_parent)
    _tree_view = builder.build()

Done! Our tree is now visible in-game and can be interacted with.

docs.title: void remove_attribute(attribute_id: String)
docs.text: Removes attribute_id from attributes and emits attribute_changed with removed set to true.

docs.title: void set_attribute_value(attribute_id: String, index: int, value: Variant)
docs.text: Updates a value at index in the attribute array for attribute_id and emits attribute_changed if the attribute and index are valid. value must be of type int or float.

docs.title: YggdrasilSerializer API
docs.text: Singleton with functions to serialize and deserialize YggdrasilTreeState

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void save_tree_state(tree: YggdrasilTree) void load_tree_state(tree: YggdrasilTree)

docs.title: void save_tree_state(tree: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Serializes YggdrasilTreeState from YggdrasilTree into user://yggdrasil/{uid}.tree file.

docs.title: void load_tree_state(tree: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Deserializes YggdrasilTreeState from user://yggdrasil/{uid}.tree file into YggdrasilTree.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilAllocationService API
docs.text: Service for allocating, preallocating, refunding, and deallocating nodes in YggdrasilTreeView.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: Name node_preallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) node_unpreallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) node_allocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) node_deallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) refund_mode_entered() refund_mode_exited() node_refund_added(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) node_refund_removed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Default Callable preallocation_check Callable() Callable allocation_check Callable() Callable deallocation_check Callable() Callable refund_check Callable() Array[int] _preallocated_nodes [] Array[int] _refund_nodes [] bool _refund_mode false

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree) void on_node_pressed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void confirm_preallocations() void clear_preallocations() void enter_refund_mode() void exit_refund_mode() void confirm_refund() bool is_refund_mode() Array[int] get_refund_nodes()

docs.title: node_preallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is marked as preallocated.

docs.title: node_unpreallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is removed from the preallocated state.

docs.title: node_allocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node becomes allocated.

docs.title: node_deallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node becomes deallocated.

docs.title: refund_mode_entered()
docs.text: Emitted when refund mode is entered.

docs.title: refund_mode_exited()
docs.text: Emitted when refund mode is exited.

docs.title: node_refund_added(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is staged for refund.

docs.title: node_refund_removed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is removed from the refund list.

docs.title: Callable preallocation_check = Callable()
docs.text: Optional callback used to validate whether preallocation is allowed.

docs.title: Callable allocation_check = Callable()
docs.text: Optional callback used to validate whether allocation is allowed.

docs.title: Callable deallocation_check = Callable()
docs.text: Optional callback used to validate whether deallocation is allowed.

docs.title: Callable refund_check = Callable()
docs.text: Optional callback used to validate whether refund staging is allowed.

docs.title: Array[int] _preallocated_nodes = []
docs.text: Stores ids of currently preallocated nodes.

docs.title: Array[int] _refund_nodes = []
docs.text: Stores ids of nodes currently staged for refund.

docs.title: bool _refund_mode = false
docs.text: Tracks whether refund mode is active.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Loads allocation state from tree_data.tree_state.allocated_nodes, marks matching nodes as allocated, updates their state to active, and emits node_allocated.

docs.title: void on_node_pressed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Handles node press behavior for allocation, preallocation, refund staging, refund unstaging, or deallocation depending on current tree settings and refund mode.

docs.title: void confirm_preallocations()
docs.text: Converts all preallocated nodes into allocated nodes, emits node_allocated for each one, and clears the preallocation list.

docs.title: void clear_preallocations()
docs.text: Clears all preallocated nodes, emits node_unpreallocated for each one, and empties the preallocation list.

docs.title: void enter_refund_mode()
docs.text: Enables refund mode, clears staged refund nodes, and emits refund_mode_entered.

docs.title: void exit_refund_mode()
docs.text: Clears staged refund nodes, emits node_refund_removed for each one, disables refund mode, and emits refund_mode_exited.

docs.title: void confirm_refund()
docs.text: Deallocates all nodes staged for refund, clears the refund list, disables refund mode, and emits refund_mode_exited.

docs.title: bool is_refund_mode()
docs.text: Returns whether refund mode is currently active.

docs.title: Array[int] get_refund_nodes()
docs.text: Returns a duplicate of the current refund node id list.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilBuilder API

docs.title: YggdrasilTreeView build()
docs.text: Creates a tree view, connects configured callbacks, adds the tree view to the parent, loads runtime tree state outside the editor, initializes the tree view, applies allocation callbacks, and returns the built tree view.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilCamera API
docs.text: Camera controller for zooming and dragging a tree view viewport within bounds.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: Name zoom_changed(zoom: float, previous_zoom: float)

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Default Control _viewport null Rect2 _bounds Rect2() float _zoom 1.0 bool _dragging false Vector2 _last_mouse_pos Vector2(0, 0)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void set_viewport(viewport: Control) void set_bounds(bounds: Rect2) void input(event: InputEvent) void set_camera_zoom(zoom: float)

docs.title: zoom_changed(zoom: float, previous_zoom: float)
docs.text: Emitted when the camera zoom changes.

docs.title: Control _viewport = null
docs.text: Viewport control affected by camera movement and zoom.

docs.title: Rect2 _bounds = Rect2()
docs.text: Bounds used to clamp camera movement.

docs.title: float _zoom = 1.0
docs.text: Current camera zoom factor.

docs.title: bool _dragging = false
docs.text: Tracks whether middle mouse dragging is active.

docs.title: Vector2 _last_mouse_pos = Vector2(0, 0)
docs.text: Last recorded mouse position while dragging.

docs.title: void set_viewport(viewport: Control)
docs.text: Sets the viewport control, enables offset transforms on it, and connects resize handling.

docs.title: void set_bounds(bounds: Rect2)
docs.text: Sets the camera movement bounds.

docs.title: void input(event: InputEvent)
docs.text: Handles mouse wheel zooming and middle mouse dragging input for the camera.

docs.title: void set_camera_zoom(zoom: float)
docs.text: Sets the camera zoom, updates viewport transform scale and position, clamps the result, and emits zoom_changed.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilConnection API
docs.text: Line node used to represent a connection between tree nodes.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilConnectionsService API
docs.text: Service for loading, creating, updating, and refreshing connections between nodes in YggdrasilTreeView.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: Name line_created(line: YggdrasilConnection, from_node_id: int, to_node_id: int) line_disconnected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node: YggdrasilNodeButton) node_connected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node_id: int) node_disconnected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node_id: int)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree) void create_connection(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node: YggdrasilNodeButton) PackedVector2Array _bezier_points(segments: int, p0: Vector2, p1: Vector2, p2: Vector2) PackedVector2Array _arc_points(segments: int, center: Vector2, reversed: bool) void update_connected_lines(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) Vector2 _get_center_position(node: Control) void on_node_allocation_changed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)

docs.title: line_created(line: YggdrasilConnection, from_node_id: int, to_node_id: int)
docs.text: Emitted when a connection line is created.

docs.title: line_disconnected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when an existing connection between two nodes is removed.

docs.title: node_connected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node_id: int)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is connected to another node.

docs.title: node_disconnected(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node_id: int)
docs.text: Emitted when a node is disconnected from another node.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Loads all stored node connections from tree_data, instantiates their line nodes, applies line data, adds them to the lines container, updates visibility based on tree reveal state, and emits connection signals.

docs.title: void create_connection(from_node: YggdrasilNodeButton, to_node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Creates a connection between from_node and to_node. If the connection already exists, removes it instead and emits disconnection signals.

docs.title: PackedVector2Array _bezier_points(segments: int, p0: Vector2, p1: Vector2, p2: Vector2)
docs.text: Returns a sampled quadratic Bézier curve point array between p0 and p2 using p1 as the control point.

docs.title: PackedVector2Array _arc_points(segments: int, center: Vector2, reversed: bool)
docs.text: Returns a sampled arc point array around center using the specified segment count and direction.

docs.title: void update_connected_lines(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Rebuilds the geometry of all incoming and outgoing lines connected to node.

docs.title: Vector2 _get_center_position(node: Control)
docs.text: Returns the center position of node based on its position and size.

docs.title: void on_node_allocation_changed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Refreshes the visual state of lines connected to node after its allocation state changes.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilDecorationsService API
docs.text: Service for loading and creating decoration nodes in YggdrasilTreeView.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: Name decoration_created(decoration: YggdrasilNodeButton) decoration_pressed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree) void create_decoration(at_position: Vector2, icon_texture: Texture2D) void create_from_prefab(position: Vector2, prefab: YggdrasilPrefab)

docs.title: decoration_created(decoration: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a decoration node is created and added to the decorations container.

docs.title: decoration_pressed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a decoration node is pressed.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Loads decoration data from tree_data.decorations, instantiates decoration nodes, configures their icons, positions them, adds them to the decorations container, and connects their pressed signal.

docs.title: void create_decoration(at_position: Vector2, icon_texture: Texture2D)
docs.text: Creates a new decoration node at at_position, adds it to the decorations container, stores its translated position in tree data, appends its node data to tree_data.decorations, and emits decoration_created.

docs.title: void create_from_prefab(position: Vector2, prefab: YggdrasilPrefab)
docs.text: Creates a decoration node from prefab, optionally associates it with the prefab reference, positions it, adds it to the decorations container, and emits decoration_created.

docs.title: YggdrasilLineData API
docs.text: Data object describing how a connection line should be drawn.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Default LineType line_type STRAIGHT float curve_height 48.0 int segments 16 bool reversed false

docs.title: enum LineType:
docs.text:
• LineType STRAIGHT = 0
    • Draws the line as a straight segment between points.
  • LineType BEZIER = 1
    • Draws the line as a quadratic Bézier curve.
  • LineType ARC = 2
    • Draws the line as an arc.

docs.title: LineType line_type = STRAIGHT
docs.text: Determines how the connection line is drawn.

docs.title: float curve_height = 48.0
docs.text: Height of the curve used for curved line types.

docs.title: int segments = 16
docs.text: Number of segments used to sample curved line types.

docs.title: bool reversed = false
docs.text: Reverses the curve or arc direction.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilLoader API
docs.text: Singleton that loads YggdrasilRegistry and provides access to registered trees.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Default YggdrasilRegistry _registry null Dictionary[String, String] _path_to_tree {}

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name YggdrasilRegistry get_registry() YggdrasilTree load_tree(path: String) void add_tree_to_registry(group: YggdrasilGroup, tree: YggdrasilTree)

docs.title: YggdrasilRegistry _registry = null
docs.text: Loaded tree registry.

docs.title: Dictionary[String, String] _path_to_tree = {}
docs.text: Maps lowercase group/tree paths to tree resource paths.

docs.title: YggdrasilRegistry get_registry()
docs.text: Returns the loaded registry.

docs.title: YggdrasilTree load_tree(path: String)
docs.text: Loads a tree by case-insensitive group/tree path, returning null and pushing an error if the path is not registered or the resource fails to load.

docs.title: void add_tree_to_registry(group: YggdrasilGroup, tree: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Adds a lowercase group/tree mapping for tree.resource_path to the runtime registry cache.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree) void delete_node(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) YggdrasilNodeButton create_node(position: Vector2, node_type: NodeType) YggdrasilNodeButton create_from_prefab(position: Vector2, prefab: YggdrasilPrefab) YggdrasilNodeButton duplicate_node(original_node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_preallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_unpreallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_allocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_deallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_refund_added(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) void on_node_refund_removed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) YggdrasilNodeButton get_node(node_id: int)

docs.title: node_created(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node button is created.

docs.title: node_pressed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Emitted when a node button is pressed.

docs.title: node_hovered(node: YggdrasilNodeButton, is_hovered: bool)
docs.text: Emitted when a node button hover state changes.

docs.title: Dictionary[int, YggdrasilNodeButton] _nodes = {}
docs.text: Maps node ids to created node button instances.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Loads all nodes from tree_data by creating button instances for each stored node resource.

docs.title: void delete_node(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Removes node from the internal node id map.

docs.title: YggdrasilNodeButton create_node(position: Vector2, node_type: NodeType)
docs.text: Creates a new node at position with node_type, adds it to the tree data and node container, connects press handling, emits node_created, and returns it.

docs.title: YggdrasilNodeButton create_from_prefab(position: Vector2, prefab: YggdrasilPrefab)
docs.text: Creates a new node from prefab at position, copies prefab visuals and data, adds it to the tree, optionally links it back to the prefab by reference id, emits node_created, and returns it.

docs.title: YggdrasilNodeButton duplicate_node(original_node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Duplicates original_node into a new node with a new id, copied visuals and attributes, offset position, optional prefab linkage, emits node_created, and returns it.

docs.title: void on_node_preallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node becomes preallocated.

docs.title: void on_node_unpreallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node is removed from the preallocated state.

docs.title: void on_node_allocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node becomes allocated.

docs.title: void on_node_deallocated(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node becomes deallocated.

docs.title: void on_node_refund_added(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node is staged for refund.

docs.title: void on_node_refund_removed(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Updates node and its neighbors after the node is removed from refund staging.

docs.title: YggdrasilNodeButton get_node(node_id: int)
docs.text: Returns the node button for node_id, or null if it is not found.

docs.title: @tool YggdrasilPrefabsService API
docs.text: Service for loading, creating, and resolving YggdrasilPrefab instances for a tree.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text: Name prefab_created(prefab: YggdrasilPrefab)

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Default Dictionary[NodeType, Array] prefabs {} Dictionary[String, YggdrasilPrefab] _ref_id_to_prefab {}

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree) YggdrasilPrefab create_prefab(node: YggdrasilNodeButton, is_copy: bool = false) void make_unique(node: YggdrasilNodeButton) YggdrasilPrefab get_prefab_by_reference_id(reference_id: String)

docs.title: prefab_created(prefab: YggdrasilPrefab)
docs.text: Emitted when a prefab is loaded or created.

docs.title: Dictionary[NodeType, Array] prefabs = {}
docs.text: Maps node types to prefab arrays.

docs.title: Dictionary[String, YggdrasilPrefab] _ref_id_to_prefab = {}
docs.text: Maps prefab reference ids to prefab instances.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree)
docs.text: Loads prefabs from tree_data, builds lookup caches, emits prefab_created for each prefab, and reconnects existing nodes to their referenced prefabs.

docs.title: YggdrasilPrefab create_prefab(node: YggdrasilNodeButton, is_copy: bool = false)
docs.text: Creates a prefab from node, optionally assigns a new reference id and links the node to it when is_copy is false, stores the prefab in tree data and lookup caches, emits prefab_created, and returns it.

docs.title: void make_unique(node: YggdrasilNodeButton)
docs.text: Removes prefab linkage from node, clearing its reference id and prefab reference.

docs.title: YggdrasilPrefab get_prefab_by_reference_id(reference_id: String)
docs.text: Returns the prefab for reference_id, or null if it is not found.

docs.text: Service responsible for loading and creating decorations.

docs.text: Service responsible for loading and creating nodes.

docs.text: Service responsible for loading and updating connections.

docs.text: Service responsible for loading and creating prefabs.

docs.text: Service responsible for allocation and refund behavior.

docs.title: PackedScene _tooltip_scene = null
docs.text: Scene used to instantiate the tooltip.

docs.title: YggdrasilTooltip _tooltip = null
docs.text: Tooltip instance used to inspect hovered nodes.

docs.title: YggdrasilTree _tree_data = null
docs.text: Loaded tree data used by this view.

docs.title: void load_tree(tree_data: YggdrasilTree, decoration_scene: PackedScene, node_scene: PackedScene, line_scene: PackedScene, tooltip_scene: PackedScene)
docs.text: Loads tree_data, stores tooltip_scene, creates internal containers, background, camera, and services, and emits tree_version_mismatch if the saved tree state version differs from the tree version.

docs.title: Transform2D get_local_space()
docs.text: Returns the transform that converts from this control space to the tree local space.

docs.title: Vector2 translate_node_position(node: Control)
docs.text: Converts a node control position into tree-space coordinates centered on the tree.

docs.title: Vector2 translate_position(pos: Vector2)
docs.text: Converts a position into tree-space coordinates centered on the tree.

docs.title: Vector2 get_mouse_position_in_tree()
docs.text: Returns the current mouse position in tree-space coordinates.

docs.title: Yggdrasil
docs.text: Namespace script holding reusable constants and preloaded resources

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Type Name Description String VERSION Plugin version String ROOT_PATH_SETTING Project setting for generated resources path String DEFAULT_ROOT_PATH Default value for Root Path setting String REGISTRY_FILENAME_SETTING Project setting for registry filename String DEFAULT_REGISTRY_FILENAME Default value for registry filename setting String GROUP_ICON Default icon from ICON_THEME to use as a Group icon in Browser String TREE_ICON Default icon from ICON_THEME to use as a Tree icon in Browser StringName ICON_THEME Default icon theme from Editor icons Array[Color] COLOR_CODES Colors used in Browser for icons YggdrasilLoader Loader Reference to YggdrasilLoader script YggdrasilSerializer Serializer Reference to YggdrasilSerializer script PackedScene MainScreen Scene for main screen editor FuzzySearch FuzzySearch Reference to FuzzySearch script UUIDGenerator UUIDGenerator Reference to UUIDGenerator script Texture2D BlankIcon Default icon for nodes PackedScene DefaultDecorationScene Default scene for created decorations PackedScene DefaultNodeScene Default scene for created nodes PackedScene DefaultLineScene Default scene for created lines PackedScene DefaultTooltipScene Default scene for node tooltip

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Type Name Description String get_registry_path Path to registry resource file String get_root_path Path to folder with generated resources String get_registry_filename Name of registry resource

docs.title: enum AllocationState:
docs.text:
• AllocationState NORMAL = 0
    • Default state.
  • AllocationState INTERMEDIATE = 1
    • Can be allocated.
  • AllocationState ACTIVE = 2
    • Is allocated.
  • AllocationState PREALLOCATED_INTERMEDIATE = 3
    • Can be preallocated.
  • AllocationState PREALLOCATED_ACTIVE = 4
    • Is preallocated.
  • AllocationState REFUND = 5
    • Is selected for deallocation.
